subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Hi, we're looking to add pallet racking to our warehouse — roughly 8,000 sq ft space. We need high-density storage for palletized goods. Can you give us a ballpark on cost and timeline?

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Hi Carla, thanks for reaching out! Pallet racking for an 8,000 sq ft space is right in our wheelhouse. Depending on ceiling height and load requirements, we're typically looking at $35,000–$65,000 installed. I'd love to schedule a site walk to get you an accurate number. Are you free this week or next?

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: That range works. We're available Thursday afternoon or Friday morning. Friday works best for us — say 10 AM?

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Perfect — Friday at 10 AM it is. I'll send a calendar invite to this address. Could you share the facility address so I can get our estimator prepped? Also, do you have any existing racking we'd be working around?

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Great, address is 123 Industrial Blvd, . No existing racking — it's a clean floor. We'd also like to discuss adding wire decking on all levels.

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Wire decking on all levels is a smart call for forklift safety — we can absolutely spec that in. We'll bring our load-capacity worksheet Friday. See you then!

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Spoke with Carla — confirmed site visit Friday 10 AM. They have a 24-ft clear height, 3 dock doors. Interested in selective racking with wire decking. Budget around $50K. Warm lead.

subject: Follow-up: Site Visit Debrief
body: Hi Carla, great meeting you on-site Friday. Your facility is a great fit for a selective racking system with 5 levels. I'm putting together the formal proposal now and will have it to you by Wednesday. In the meantime, any questions come up?

subject: Follow-up: Site Visit Debrief
body: Thanks Jay. One question — do you handle the seismic engineering sign-off or do we need to hire someone separately?

subject: Follow-up: Site Visit Debrief
body: Great question. We handle everything in-house including the seismic engineering and permit drawings — that's included in our quote. No separate hires needed on your end. Proposal coming Wednesday.

subject: Initial Project Inquiry
body: Hi, came across your company online. We have a warehouse project we're trying to get off the ground and wanted to get a quote. Can you help?

subject: Initial Project Inquiry
body: Hi Tanya, thanks for reaching out to Pacific Construction! We'd love to learn more about your project. Could you share a bit more about what you're working on — scope, timeline, and facility location? That'll help us figure out the best way to help.

subject: Initial Project Inquiry
body: Sure — we're looking at adding storage capacity to our existing facility. Haven't pinned down the exact approach yet but budget is probably $30–$50K.

subject: Initial Project Inquiry
body: That budget is very workable. There are a few directions we could go — pallet racking, a small mezzanine, or shelving systems. Each has pros and cons depending on your SKU mix and workflow. I'd suggest a free 30-minute site visit so I can see the space and give you an educated recommendation. Does that sound reasonable?

subject: Initial Project Inquiry
body: Initial call with Tanya at Okafor Industrial LLC. Friendly conversation — they're early in the process. No specific timeline. Will send a follow-up email with our portfolio and project examples to keep them warm.

subject: Initial Project Inquiry
body: Hi Tanya, great chatting earlier! I've attached a few project examples from similar facilities we've outfitted. When you're ready to move forward on that site visit, just reply here or call me directly at 253.826.2727. Looking forward to helping you maximize that space.

subject: Structural Mezzanine — New Office Space
body: We're interested in adding a mezzanine over our existing operations floor — roughly 2,000 sq ft of usable office space. Steel structure preferred. What's your typical timeline and cost range?

subject: Structural Mezzanine — New Office Space
body: Hi John, a 2,000 sq ft structural steel mezzanine is a great investment. Depending on live load requirements and stair configuration, installed cost typically runs $80,000–$140,000. Timeline from permit approval is usually 6–8 weeks. Would a preliminary site visit work this week?

subject: Structural Mezzanine — New Office Space
body: Yes — we're open Tuesday or Wednesday. Wednesday afternoon is ideal.

subject: Structural Mezzanine — New Office Space
body: Wednesday afternoon confirmed. I'll have our structural estimator along. A few things to prep: clear height from floor to roof structure, any existing utilities that may run through the footprint, and the intended use (office, storage, or mixed)?

subject: Structural Mezzanine — New Office Space
body: Clear height is 28 ft. Some overhead conduit but nothing major. It'll be mixed use — small offices plus storage overflow.

subject: Structural Mezzanine — New Office Space
body: 28 ft gives us great headroom to work with. Mixed use is totally doable — we'll spec the floor loading accordingly. See you Wednesday!

subject: Structural Mezzanine — New Office Space
body: Post site-visit call with John. They loved the proposal concept. Want to include a full stair with handrail and one access gate. Budget approved internally up to $120K. Moving to formal proposal stage.

body: Hi David, great meeting you on-site Friday. Your facility is a great fit for a selective racking system with 5 levels. I'm putting together the formal proposal now and will have it to you by Wednesday. In the meantime, any questions come up?

subject: Follow-up: Site Visit Debrief
body: Thanks Jay. One question — do you handle the seismic engineering sign-off or do we need to hire someone separately?

subject: Follow-up: Site Visit Debrief
body: Great question. We handle everything in-house including the seismic engineering and permit drawings — that's included in our quote. No separate hires needed on your end. Proposal coming Wednesday.

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Hi, we're looking to add pallet racking to our warehouse — roughly 8,000 sq ft space. We need high-density storage for palletized goods. Can you give us a ballpark on cost and timeline?

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Hi Jennifer, thanks for reaching out! Pallet racking for an 8,000 sq ft space is right in our wheelhouse. Depending on ceiling height and load requirements, we're typically looking at $35,000–$65,000 installed. I'd love to schedule a site walk to get you an accurate number. Are you free this week or next?

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: That range works. We're available Thursday afternoon or Friday morning. Friday works best for us — say 10 AM?

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Perfect — Friday at 10 AM it is. I'll send a calendar invite to this address. Could you share the facility address so I can get our estimator prepped? Also, do you have any existing racking we'd be working around?

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Great, address is 123 Industrial Blvd, Spokane. No existing racking — it's a clean floor. We'd also like to discuss adding wire decking on all levels.

subject: Pallet Racking Quote Request
body: Wire decking on all levels is a smart call for forklift safety — we can absolutely spec that in. We'll bring our load-capacity worksheet Friday. See you then!
